gloss:
Statement of record: `Eidometry.Thesis.forcing_principle` (Eidometry thesis, section Forcing).

Given a complete lattice of candidate distinguishability structures on a state space, a set of structures that comply with an observation specification (closed under taking suprema), and a readability relation that is preserved toward finer structures (whatever a coarser structure can read, every finer structure below it can also read), there is a single greatest compliant structure, and anything readable at that greatest structure is readable under every compliant structure. The greatest compliant structure is fixed by the specification alone.

Discharged by [MTH.R-2026-1172].

statement.pretty: ∀ {S : Type u_1} {V : Type u_2} {Str : Type u_3} [inst : CompleteLattice Str] (G : WMSpec.Grammar.ObservationGrammar S V Str), IsGreatest (WMSpec.Grammar.ObservationGrammar.Compliant G) (WMSpec.Grammar.ObservationGrammar.forced G) ∧ ∀ {T : S → V}, WMSpec.Grammar.ObservationGrammar.Readable G (WMSpec.Grammar.ObservationGrammar.forced G) T → ∀ {d : Str}, d ∈ WMSpec.Grammar.ObservationGrammar.Compliant G → WMSpec.Grammar.ObservationGrammar.Readable G d T

title: Eidometry.Thesis.forcing_principle
